sections.title: Purpose
sections.content:
Note: In the event of conflict between the English and domestic language, the English language shall take precedence
This specification establishes the Outside Door Handle Assembly (also referred to as handle', draw style) component requirements for use in passenger cars, vans, and and other body styles engineered by Groupe STELLANTIS.  Minimum requirements for the performance, function and reliability as well as the corresponding validation tests are identified.

sections.title: System development context
sections.content:
The handle is a component of the door subsystem providing the function of releasing the door (swing) for ingress/egress from the exterior of the vehicle.  Additionally, the handle is used to pull the door into the open position to allow entry/exit.  The handle may also serve as the location for passive entry system electronics, NFC (Near Field Communication) and key cylinder packaging for locking and unlocking the vehicle.  The handle shall provide these functions under various environmental conditions and above all shall meet specified safety requirements.
Testing requirements noted in this specification require physical tests to be run in a defined sequence of environmental conditions, with a defined cycle count in each environment, in an attempt to approximate actual usage conditions, while balancing the supplier's (and STELLANTIS) testing time, resources, and expenses with realized benefit.
However, the product shall function as specified, in any of the specified environments, at the time of delivery.
Rocking handle, called Module 2, is an external handle develop by Stellantis and the suppliers that must comply with worldwide component and vehicle standards, regulations and requirements of national governments. This includes customs requirements of any government.
Module 2 external handle needs to fulfill worldwide requirements as will be sold in NA and European Country.

sections.title: Bracket content
sections.content: The bracket will be compatible with to different door panel material (steel and aluminum)

sections.title: Design constraints
sections.content:
Final design to work out with Stellantis design and engineering.
The supplier have to identify if any of the solution proposed is protected by patent (for example: Inertial system…).
And additionally the supplier have to certify that is not using any third party patented solution.

sections.title: Functional volume
sections.content:
The supplier proposal must fulfill the limits and interfaces of the Functional volume provided by Stellantis.
The bracket will be compatible with to different door panel material (stell and aluminium)

sections.title: Montage/Assembly
sections.content:
Assembly the cable (STELLANTIS plant) in the handle bracket:
Assembly force of the sleeve Abutment (embout de gaine) in the bracket interface without humidity: 40N Maxi as per direction F12.
Pull-out force of the sleeve abutment of the cable out of the bracket without humidity: 40N mini as per -F12
Pull-out force of the sleeve abutment of the cable out of the bracket with humidity: 20N mini as per -F12 (increased humidity will be as follows: 10 days at 40˚C and 95% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature, in accordance with procedure D471309.
For the following clipping manipulations:
A loss of maximum 20% from the 1st clipping is allowed after 5 clipping & unclipping operations.(with and without Humidity)
Assembly the bracket and the handle on the door (STELLANTIS plant):
Insert the bracket in the inner panel of the door and pre-clip the bracket by pre-cilp hooks.
Then hooks are engaged on the door panel by horizontal travel. Pre-retention must be guaranteed by the hook design, and the same without locking cable and without the operator's intervention.
Gliding force of the pre-retention hooks on the door panel is 50N max.
Insert the lock cylinder or socket into the bracket designated location.
Insert the handle through the external door panel with a rotating motion. Then push the handle in order to engage the handle hook on the bracket and link the electrical connector (where present)
Gliding force of handle may not exceed 40N +/-20 %
Tightening of the bracket eccentric screws to ensure the packaging between handle and bracket: torque screw  4 Nm +/-15 % - speed 600 RPM (screw and other fixation characteristics are defined in APOQUA file: TBD)
Tightening of the bracket lateral screws to ensure the lock cylinder fixation: torque screw  4 Nm +/-15 % - speed 600 RPM (screw and other fixation characteristics are defined in APOQUA file: TBD). The screw's tightening torque in the delivered position (on the metal clamp) must be less than the minimum fixation torque value and must be sufficient to prevent it from moving during transport.
Tightening of the bracket internal screws (screw with tolerance recovery system) to ensure the packaging between handle door panel: torque screw  4 Nm +/-15 % - speed 600 RPM (screw and other fixation characteristics are defined in APOQUA file: TBD)
After Sales/Service on the bracket clamp. Assembly Bezel (only driver door)
The lock cylinder or socket must be retained on the bracket until clamp in complete engaged (without pressing)
Other requirements:
- Give preference to clipping fasteners, various technical solutions are available from our suppliers.
- The tightening torques used in the Stellantis plants must comply with standard B13 3116.
- The choice of fasteners will be made by Stellantis.
- In the case of fixing by metric screw, a metal insert must be provided to ensure no creep.
- The Stellantis screws are with Pilot ends, so take this into account on the implantation length.
- In the case of fixing by soft materials screws (MT), the interface on the part must respect
-Tapping torque <1 NM for an MT4.5 screw (<2 NM for an MT6)
-Tightening torque : 2 NM +/- 15% for an MT 4.5 screw (4 NM +/- 15% for an MT6)

sections.title: Snap Back noise
sections.content:
For these tests following equipment is needed:
a.  a microphone to measure the noise
b.  a mono-axis accelerometer to determine the average impact speed of the handle
This test has to be performed on a vehicle door (completely fitted). If not available for DV/PV Testing, a rigid frame work or a sheet metal jig can be used as well. For MRD EL1, a complete assembled vehicle door shall be used.
Acceptance is done across the whole audible frequency range (20HZ - 20KHZ) for a duration of 10s. Test to be performed 6 times in order to get representative results.
See sketch below for test set up, the measurement axis of the accelerometer is following the movement of the handle.
Picture 5.0 shows the test set up.
Picture 5.0
Analysis of acoustic data:
The analysis of the acoustic data can be done with:
a.  integration time : impulse
b.  weighting A
Analysis of vibratory data:
The acceleration measures allow the average impact speed of the handle. This speed is measured from the temporal signal issued by the accelerometer and it is determined in the following manner:
a.  Note the time Δt between release and hit of the door skin (Figure 4)
Divide the movement of the door skin (Graph 5.0) by the time Δt
Graph 5.0
Target dB(A) is max. 70, snap back speed by spring.

sections.title: Handle operation: Vibration, Squeak and Rattle
sections.content: The requirement for the handle is to have absolutely no customer annoyance due to squeaks and rattles. There shall be no squeaks or rattles at any of the interfacing or contact points between parts. There shall be no squeaks or rattles between the cable end fitting and the interface to the handle assembly. The Supplier is to accept full responsibility to produce squeak and rattle free, subsystems and/or components.  They shall cooperate with STELLANTIS and/or other supplier to assure that no squeak and rattle annoyance occurs at the interface of their subsystem/component to the body, chassis or other subsystem / component.  If an objectionable noise is found during any phase of the program, it will be the supplier's responsibility, with STELLANTIS cooperation, to eliminate the noise and demonstrate resolution in a vehicle prior to exiting the program phase.  If the noise is found in pilot or production, the supplier will be responsible to develop a containment plan (and fix), as well a short and long term solutions, as appropriate.  Any issue raised during these processes, which is demonstrated to be the responsibility of the supplier, shall be corrected with urgency at no additional cost to STELLANTIS.  The Supplier will be responsible for the Squeak & Rattle performance of their subsystem.

sections.content:
This measurement has to be performed in a small cabine, matching the handle size. Objective 0dB from 400Hz to 10kHz.
Purpose of test is to determine noise reduction effiency of the handle gaskets and other provisions.
Measurement to be performed in a small cabine according to STELLANTIS norm 00978_13_00762, the handle assembled on a small (cabin matching) sheet metal panel (0,65mm thickness) with a bracket (but without peripheral interface parts as inner panel, door trim i.e.)
Criteria: 0dB between 400Hz and 10kHz.
The delta between a measured sheet metal and a door handle installed on a datacontrol model should be 0 dB
The performance must come out above the curve criterion.
Any fault (surface below the curve criterion) must be <= 15 points.
Falling below the criterion by a third of an octave must be <= 3 dB

sections.title: Aeroacoustics noise (Stellantis Vehicle Test)
sections.content:
The physical features are evaluated in an aero-acoustic wind tunnel on a standard or prototype vehicle. This vehicle is submitted to a frontal wind or to skidding (wind impact equal to + or - 10° in relation to the X axis of the vehicle), and in several conditions:
Maximum condition: vehicle in condition whereby all external accessories are taken off and all the joints and chassis parts taped down.
Characterisation the condition of a watertight zone or accessory: maximum condition of vehicle whereby tape is taken off the watertight zone or for which the accessory is remounted.
These states are characterised in the following manner: measures of interior noise done by a wind of 140 km/h under an impact of 0°and of +/-10° on Head Acoustics dummies placed on the seats arranged at half-way between X and Z and operated by internal or external ear according to the source position.
Thus, for each state and for each impact corresponding to spectrums of noise in thirds of octaves and fine bands (units of measure and references dB(A)).
Maximum state :
-  maximum noise condition: algebraic sum, by frequency bands 1/3 octave (400 Hz < f  < 10 000 Hz), of the dB at the level of acoustic pressure of the vehicle
-  sensitivity to skidding of maximum condition: difference between maximum noise condition at  +/-10° and that at 0°.
Criteria for sealing and accessories:
The watertight zones and accessories are characterised by twoo types of criteria:
-  participation: algebraic sum of dB or distance points of the condition in relation to maximum condition at 140 km/h under impact of 0°and of +/-10° ;
-  sensitivity to skidding: difference between participation at +/-10° and that at 0° ;

sections.title: Spurious noise
sections.content:
The handle must not emit static noise (requirement B284185-009)
To simulate the efficency of static noise reduction/avoidance, 2 tests must be done during the project:
a.  complete handle mounted on a representative door skin with depression in prototype stage
b.  handle mounted on a door equipped with window panes, grooves and polishers ( to be provided by STELLANTIS, meet milestone VRS2DV).
-  All gaskets to be present.
-  Single components on bracket (i.e. counterweight, release lever and all other moveable parts) isolated in the way that they cannot create a noise (would disturb the test results)
-  The risk of not allowed frequencies must be taken into account in the development stage: no part included must have a resonance frequency “stuck” on resonance frequencies/own support mode.
-  Tool markings (splitlines, ejector pin i.e.) must not cause noises during handling of the HANDLE, they must not be positioned in contact with mobile parts.
Validation of the HANDLE must be done by static noise measures which are compared to the acceptance.
a.  Subjective listening must also be done in independent of the test result.
b.  Subjective litening by Stellantis experts
DESCRIPTION OF THE STATIC NOISE TEST:
As shown by the requirement - Acoustic response spectrum maximum 1/3 octave when new -, it is imperative to respect the following test chronology:
a.  Before installing a part: Application of the temporal listening stimulation profile and proceeding to acceptance of the environmental acoustic response + mounting for comparison with the - acoustic response spectrum maximum 1/3 octave when empty (validation of test conditions without the part to analyse)
b.  When new: Application of the temporal listening stimulation profile and proceeding to acceptance of the acoustic response of the part for comparison with the graph specified in the requirement - acoustic response spectrum maximum 1/3 octave when new.
c.  Ageing on handling: Submit the HANDLE to 3 years climatic endurance handling (corresponding to a number of cyles G1G2 = 55000 cycles)
d.  After ageing during handling: application of the temporal listening stimulation profile and proceeding to acceptance of the acoustic response of the part for comparison with the graph specified in the requirement - acoustic response spectrum maximum 1/3 octave on use.
e.  Vibration ageing: Application of the stimulation profile creating vibration fatigue (this profile corresponds to an equivalent of 3 years). The stimulation profile creating vibratory fatigue is a temporal signal of ageing of 117 hours defining a response representing damage sustained by the body when it is integrated into the vehicle (with compulsory sorting at simultaneous axes)
At the end of the test: Application of the temporal listening stimulation profile and proceeding to acceptance of the acoustic response of the part for comparison with the graph specified in the requirement - Spectrum acoustic response spectrum maximum 1/3 octave on use.
The part is submitted to several temporal listening stimulations representing response stimulations measured on the STELLANTIS static noise tracks. (This signal will be updated by STELLANTIS during the development of the project). The listening signal applied to the HANDLE allows the absence of noise sensed by the occupants of the vehicle to be judged.
All Stored data to submit STELLANTIS after end of the test in excel native format. See an example of a detailed protocol of this test in appendix B/chapter 9

sections.title: Water-tightness/retention and freeze test
sections.content:
The gaskets have to be designed in a way to limit water intrusion into the inside of the door.
The cable routing has to be designed to avoid any risk of water accumulation/infiltration inside it.
A specific water test according Q548200 will be performed at STELLANTIS engineering. In case of performance issues, STELLANTIS expect suppliers support for improvements.
The handle shall pass the following water freeze test (handle is connected to the Latch with Cable)
Mount the handle to a simulated outer door panel coupon in body position with a drip panel used to simulate water entry inside the door (see picture 5.1)
2.  Flow water to the following specifications:
a.  Hose Size 16.0 mm inside diameter
b.  Hose Location 100+/- 13.0 mm from the drip panel
c.  Spray the drip panel ledge in the area of the handle for 10 seconds
d.  Flow rate 0.15 liters per second
e.  Duration of spray 10.0 +/1 seconds
Picture 5.1
3.  Use a power car wash' type nozzle, which is capable of providing a flat spray pattern with a 40º dispersion angle and 9.5 liter/minute volume flow rate at a 4,825 kPa/48 bar pressure.  The nozzle should be positioned 300 +/- 13.0 mm from face of handle.  Allow the spray to strike the entire handle.  Duration of spray is 20.0 +/- 2.0 seconds.  Place the panel and handle into the cold chamber within 90 seconds of the start of the test.
4.  Cool the handle assembly and panel for 2 hours minimum at -40 +/- 5º C.
5.  With the handle at the above temperature, pull the handle at the center and perpendicular to the surface; it shall take no more than 160N static load to break any ice lock' of the handle assembly.  When the handle is released, it shall return to a position that allows the latching system to fully function.  There shall be no breakage or damage to the handle.  Any noise caused by ice or ice build- up shall be noted in the test report.
6.  After the handle system warms to ambient temperature, the handle shall return to pre-test rest position and condition.
Note:  Resistive load (representing friction of the latch and system = 35 N) shall be applied in one direction to the handle.

sections.title: Automatic Car Wash
sections.content:
The handle shall be designed to pass through an automatic car wash without damage to the exterior parts when contacted by moving car wash brushes and dryers.
a.  Components shall resist the force of high-pressure spray equipment
b.  Parts shall be designed to avoid catching brushes/cleaning media
c.  There shall be no inadvertent activation of the handle during automated car washes
d.  Cleaning procedures and recommended materials for cleaning shall be identified and listed in the owner's manual
e.  Recesses, crevices, or enclosures in components shall be easily accessible for cleaning
f.  Components shall not hold water via pooling or capillary action
g.  Grained parts shall have a wax release grain

sections.title: Conditions of increased humidity
sections.content:
All tests must be done under the following pre-condions : 10 days at 40˚C and 95% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (in accordance with procedure D471309).
Except if different or specific conditions are required.
The “standard” conditions of increased humidity representative of client use and to be taken into account for all tests are those defined in the ST Plastic Materials PMXP_ECH08_1340, being 10 days at 40˚C and 95% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (in accordance with procedure D471309).
In case of emergency actions (to be agreed with STELLANTIS engineering), the specified 10 day can be reduced to following values:
a.  96 hours at 70˚C 95% humidity
b.  2 hours at ambient temperature
This test set up cannot not be used for product validation.
Special case for parts assembled in the STELLANTIS factory:
For all tests relating to parts assembly (clipping the bezel on to the lock cylinder, for example), increased humidity of 5 days at 23˚C and 50% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (in accordance with procedure D471309) has to be performed. In these conditions, the criteria specific to the ST must be satisfied. Besides this, the tests will be repeated for dry parts (such as is defined in the norm NF T58-000, being 48 h after injection in an ambience stabilised at 23˚C and 50% relative humidity). In this case, the force variations must be less than 15%.
For unclipping tests, the conditions of increased humidity will be as follows: 10 days at 40˚C and 95% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (in accordance with procedure D471309). Please note that for unclipping tests of the bezel for the lock cylinder and cable dethatching the parts will be placed in the depths of the oven assembled on a mounting which is representative of the vehicle situation (part of door panel)

sections.title: Rigidity and resistance
sections.content:
Test conditions before testing:
The tests have to be performed with a original door sheet metal or if not available, a simulated door skin to use.
For all tests before testing, the free play and the cable travel has to be measured and compared with values after the tests. As well:
a.  All parts tested for strength will be tested to failure at the specified temperature (consider two different batches for strenght test and strength test up to failure)
b.  Handle loads at failure must meet or exceed specified strength test load.
Mode of failure and test load at failure must be documented.

sections.title: Strength Test - Full Open Position
sections.content:
The handle in the full open position shall withstand a 667 N force when pulled perpendicular to the handle (see picture 5.3) in the F1 direction at a temperature -40°C and +23°C (humidity uncontrolled). No breaking of the handle (especially in the hand insertion area), nor the handle coming loose from the bracket is permitted. Perform test + 85°C for information only (the test report shall be reviewed with the appropriate STELLANTIS personnel for determination of whether further action is required). The moving grip part of the handle must firmly return to its rest position.
Under a load of 100 N, max. displacement of the handle in F1 direction of 1,5 mm at 23°C and 2 mm at 85°C is permitted.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)

sections.title: Strength Test- Inertia lock manually engaged
sections.content:
The handle shall withstand a 355 N load in the F1 direction with the handle lever blocked at the rod/cable connect point with the inertia locker.  Test to be performed at -40°C, 23°C and 85°C in F1 direction (see picture 5.3) Displacement of the handle max. 2 mm. No breaking of the handle, nor the handle coming loose from the bracket is permitted, no degradation of electronic performance.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)

sections.title: Strength Test - Horizontal Load at handle's vertex - Full open
sections.content:
For this test, apply a load of 100N on “point 1” (see picture 5.7) in F1 direction (with handle in full open position) and measure the the displacement of handle on “point 2” in -F1 direction.
Repeat the same test applying the load on “point 2” and measure the displacement of handle on “point 1”
Under a load of 100 N, max. displacement on “point 1 and 2” of the handle in -F1 direction of 2 mm at 23°C and 3 mm at 85°C is permitted.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)
Picture 5.7

sections.title: Strength Test - Longitudinal Load-Rest Position
sections.content:
For this test, apply a load of 500N in F4/-F4 direction (see picture 5.2) in the temperatures ranges -40°C, 23°C and 85°C (humidity uncontrolled). No breaking of the handle, nor the handle coming loose from the bracket is permitted, no degradation of electronic performance.
Under a load of 100 N, max. displacement of the handle in F4/-F4 direction of 0,5 mm at 23°C and 1 mm at 85°C is permitted.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)

sections.title: Strength Test - Longitudinal Load-Full Open Position
sections.content:
Test to be performed at -40°C, 23°C and 85°C (humidity uncontrolled) in F4/-F4 (see picture 5.2) direction with a force of 500N in the shown areas applied. No breaking of the handle, nor the handle coming loose from the bracket is permitted, no degradation of electronic performance.
Under a load of 100 N, max. displacement of the handle in F4/-F4 direction of 1 mm at 23°C and 1,5 mm at 85°C is permitted.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)

sections.title: Stiffness of the HANDLE to an horizontal load, handle at rest
sections.content:
This test represents a user leaning on his vehicle on the COE. The COE must resist -30°C to +85°C to a -F1 stress of 500 N (see picture 5.2/5.3). After this test, no breakage, nor permanent distortion, nor deterioration, nor loss of functions, of the COE is permitted. The cable pull (§ 5.2.4.2) will remain compliant with the requirements of the plan.
Under a load of 50 N, max. displacement of the handle in -F1 direction of 1 mm at 23°C and 1,5 mm at 85°C is permitted.
(different batches to be used for each temperature)

sections.title: Resistance and rigidity of the bracket when flexed
sections.content:
During a lateral impact (regulated or high-frequency), the intrusion of the barrier and possible hit of the handle , impacts and distorts the entire handle/bracket assembly. In order to avoid excessive cable pull caused by the attacked bracket, the bracket has to meet following requirements. Test set ups acc. pictures 5.10/5.11/5.12 , to be reviewed and approved by STELLANTIS engineering.
Picture 5.10
a.  All parts tested for strength will be tested to failure at the specified temperature 23°C Humidity uncontrolled.
b.  Handle loads at failure must meet or exceed specified strength test load.
c.  Mode of failure and test load at failure must be documented.
d.  With door handle, cable and sheet metal (also 3th fixation on bracket must be foreseen)
Two Robustness test on the bracket to perform:

sections.title: Load on the front of the bracket
sections.content:
Follow instructions according to the test setup description below:
Picture 5.11

sections.title: Load on the rear of the bracket
sections.content:
Follow instructions according to the test setup description below:
Picture 5.12
For each of the 2 load application, 3 different test to perform

sections.title: Traction Load
sections.content:
During lateral impact, the barrier can pull the handle during bouncing.
a.  All parts tested for strength will be tested to failure at the specified temperature.
b.  Handle loads at failure must meet or exceed specified strength test load.
c.  Mode of failure and test load at failure must be documented.

sections.title: Static traction load on the handle
sections.content:
The handle will be mounted on a sheet metal bench and fixed with the screw and key cylinder (test to perform as well with the socket) via the clamp. The inertia locker has to be in engaged state if irreversibility function is taken.
With a load of 700 N applied  (speed 50 mm/min) in F1 direction as shown in the picture 5.2/5.3 (starting from rotated position allowed by the inertial locker), no permanent deflection of the bracket/handle is permitted. Cable movement max. 4.5 mm.

sections.title: Dynamic traction load on the handle
sections.content:
Same requirements as in chapter 6.2.4.4.8.1 but dynamic force to be applied by a kind of negative hammer. Hight of the tube and mass of the weight to calculate in order to reach the 700N.
Picture 5.20

sections.title: Other requirements
sections.content:
The handle must remain fully functional during door slam testing at STELLANTIS.
The handle must remain functional in all tests on vibrating silencer.

sections.title: Function check in vibration testing
sections.content:
Two tests defined to verify the vibration resistance:
a)  A synthesized impact test from the response spectrum to the impact measured on the vehicle, Value to be provided from STELLANTIS engineering. If not available, use following test:
b)  Simulation of the HANDLE by sinusoidal acceleration.
Test set up to be agreed with STELLANTIS engineering.
In general, 2 tests to perform:

sections.title: Handle is instrumented in a position of displacement:
sections.content:
a)  Fix the sensitive part (capable of leading to non-function, example : blocking the handle in its kinematics) on the driver (example : shaker) to accelerate in the Y direction of the vehicle,
b)  Record the displacement and acceleration of the handle,
c)  Steer the driver in displacement :
d)  Variable sinusoidal signal from 20 to 40 Hz, logarithmic scanning, 5 second duration, amplitude 10 mm.
e)  Check the level of acceleration leading to non-function.

sections.title: Handle is not instrumented:
sections.content:
a)  Fix the sensitive part on to the driver to accelerate in the Y direction of the vehicle,
b)  Record the acceleration of the HANDLE,
c)  Steer the driver in displacement :
d)  Variable sinusoidal signal from 20 to 40 Hz, logarithmic scanning, 5 second duration, amplitude 5 mm.
e)  Increase the amplitude of displacement by a step of 0.25 mm,
f)  After each scanning check the maximum acceleration level of the HANDLE and the level of acceleration leading to non-function.
g)  The displacement amplitude could be adapted by means of the acceleration levels advocated for the HANDLE.

sections.title: Tight fit of gasket
sections.content: The gasket must stay in position during shipment and assembly operation.

sections.title: Performance of the tightening fixing torque
sections.content:
With the handle mounted on a vehicle configuration framework, screws are tightened to 4 Nm +/-15 % (speed - TBC).
After being put through extreme temperatures of (-30°C and +85°C), i.e. 4 AF cycles in accordance with D47 1309, there can be no loss of coupling greater than 20%
Verification with following procedure (has to be done also post durability test (see chapter 6.6.1.2):
a. Before start of the durability tests, tightening of the screws at the nominal value of 4Nm with a calibrated screw driver
b. Mark (with a line) the angular position of the screw
c. Post-test, measure torque (with same screwdriver) and communicate as well any displacement (difference from b/c) to Stellantis engineering. Torque to be in specification (4 Nm +/- 15%).
Second point:
After a minimum of 5 assemblies and 5 disassembly's, we should be able to carry out tightening of screws to a coupling of 4 Nm +/- 15 % (no deterioration of the front and back tappings).

sections.title: Beauty cover performance
sections.content:
Beauty cover or any other component of handle must not detached during testing under any circumstance.
When the client touches the beauty cover:
-  No creak noise or other are accepted
-  The handle needs to give the impression of robustness by the design proposed by the supplier

sections.title: Drop Test (to be performed with B21 7120 M3.3 free fall)
sections.content:
Drop test requirement to determine with prototype parts.
After being dropped from a height of 1 meter onto a concrete floor, the components must not show any signs of breakage, nor function faults, including ADML and NFC.  If this requirement cannot be met, the ensemble must show visible faults (splinters, cracks, …) for the operator. Inertia blocker not engaged and bracket to stay in pre stage condition.

sections.title: Axis holding - To avoid the axis getting out of place.
sections.content:
The articulation axis, must resist to an extraction effort/stress during 5 second by +/- F « axis direction » of 300N minimum at -30°C , 23°C and at 85°C, the extraction effort must resist to 150N.
At the end of the test the displacement of the axis, must be lower than 0,5mm compared to the initial position.

sections.title: Performance on impact
sections.content:
The handle/bracket system has to meet all legal requirements listed in chapter 3.2.1, External Standards/Specifications. All tests to be performed at Stellantis at vehicle level with the exception of ECE11, FMVSS206, ECE26 and 2002/95/EC. Those 4 requirements have to be tested at supplier. Regardless of test responsibility, chapter 6.6 will point out, how the supplier has to support.
All calculations have to be performed with worst case conditions (i.e. springs on lowest or highest end of torque tolerance, counterweight with min or max specified mass, with justification) in order to meet legal requirements.

sections.title: Side impact
sections.content:
With lateral impact, three phases are identified and can cause an unallowed opening of the side door.
a)  Phase 1:
From 0ms to 20ms: acceleration in both directions of Y (door bouncing/backlash).
b)  Phase 2:
From 20 ms to 70 ms : severe deformation of the door (as well in handle location area
c)  Phase 3:
From 70 ms to 300 ms: barrier pullback, during this event, the barrier can pull the handle and cause a door opening.
During all identified phases, the door has to remain closed according to the legal requirements.
In the following chapters the blocking system will be called: “inertia blocker”, “inertial system”, or “inertia lock”
The inertial system shall be:
- Option 1 - Request for EU market. Irreversible. That means that the counterweight remains blocked by the system after the crash (inertia blocker engaged).
- Option 2 - Request for NA market. Reversible. As the counterweight is not blocked anymore after the crash, this option is only possible if there's no risk of door opening by the barrier during phases 2 and 3 for all vehicle applications. Option not compliant with modular policy
- Component design synergy between option 1 and 2 needs to be studied
- Option 3 - Any other design (to define) which can guarantee modular applicability (system works for many different vehicles) + North America reversibility obligation specificity

sections.title: Regulation test ECER11 / FMVSS206
sections.content:
Refer to official latest release of the regulations. As a part of the latching system (cable/latch) the handle has to fulfil the 30g requirement (maintained during 30ms) and must support that the doors are fully latched during the impact. Validation must to be performed virtual point of view (calculation according to official procedure present in the regulations) and with physical test (the handle must be fully functional).
For acceleration applied in directions -X,+X and -Y,+Y, of amplitude less than or equal to 45 g (maintained to 30 ms), the HANDLE must not cause cable pull higher than or equal to 4,5 mm.
After acceleration of 45 g maintained to 30 ms, the handle must remain fully functional and supports opening of the door.
The origin of the specification is linked to the current means of approval (catapult with elastomer receptacles), which, in order to obtain the signal of 30 g for 30 ms, presents a function peak of 42 g.
Validation:
The regulation test on the door with all components (latch/cable/handle) is Stellantis's responsibility.
Component simulation and physical testing in order to meet those requirements is supplier's responsibility. Physical testing has to be performed with a kind of certified “catapult” which, in order to obtain the signal of 30 g for 30 ms, presents a function peak of 42 g

sections.title: Acceleration
sections.content:
I)  Acceleration value
The acceleration values mentioned are values filtered to 300 Hz (low pass filter with cutting frequency of 300 Hz/ CFC180). Four zones are distinguished (see graph 6.2):
a)  Zone A: The handle must not cause opening of the door during impact, but must allow opening of the door after the impact. This zone corresponds to the requirements in chapter 6.2.4.6.2 (Regulation test ECER11 / FMVSS206).
b)  Zone B: Intermediate zone, the handle must not cause opening of the door during impact. After the impact the handle may or may not allow the door to open. This zone allows different inertias to be covered for the inertia blocking system which keeps the handle non-functional/blocked. This zone is detailed below.
c)  Zone C: Functioning zone during impact: the handle must not cause opening of the door during impact for strong accelerations and does not require the handle fully functional after the crash. In the case when the barrier can pull the handle during retraction (phase 3), the handle has to stay blocked (if irreversibility function is taken). This zone is detailed below.
d)  Zone D: Zone beyond impact : zone which is situated beyond the possible test methods known by our services, no requirement in this zone and shown only for reference.
Graph 6.2
Zone A specification:
See Graph 6.2, yellow field
Zone B specification: Validation of the upper graph limit (in red)
For the following semi-sinusoidal acceleration, applied in Y direction (direction of impact), the handle must not cause cable pull higher than or equal to 4,5mm. This value must take into consideration all the different factors occurring during the test (Part deformation, stiffness…), that could generate an increasing of the cable pulling. In all cases, the addition of all the constraints, will never generate a cable pull higher to 4,5mm
a)  amplitude 80 g and half-period 30 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
b)  amplitude 80 g and half-period 10 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
c)  amplitude 100 g and half-period 5 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
d)  amplitude 150 g and half-period 3 ms (validation by calculation and physical test).
If irreversibility function taken, after the tests/requirements listed above, the handle must remain in blocked/non-functional condition, inertia locker activated. As this system keeps the handle non-functional it must therefore be activated somewhere in the acceleration range corresponding to zone B and whatever the acceleration level.
Specification of zone C: Validation of the higher graph limit (in black)
For acceleration in the Y direction, semi-sinusoidal as follows :
a)  amplitude 450 g and half-period 10 ms (calculation validation only requested),
b)  amplitude 520 g and half-period 3 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
c)  amplitude 450 g and half-period 4 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
d)  amplitude 350 g and half-period 5 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
e)  amplitude 150 g and half-period 10 ms (validation by calculation and physical test),
The handle must not cause cable traction higher than or equal to 4,5mm. This value must take into consideration all the different factors occurring during the test (Part deformation, stiffness…), that could generate an increasing of the cable pulling. In all cases, the addition of all the constraints, will never generate a cable pull higher to 4,5mm
Validation of zone C: Validation of the total surface
For the acceleration mentioned below, in the Y direction, the handle must not cause cable pull of higher than or equal to 4,5mm
To demonstrate handle system capability, supplier must perform  the following simulation :
a)  half-period of 3ms : amplitudes of 150 g, 200 g, 300 g, 400 g and 520 g,
b)  half-period of 5ms : amplitudes of 100 g, 150 g, 200 g, 300 g and 350 g,
c)  half-period of 10ms : amplitudes of 80 g, 100 g and 150 g.
Maintain of the “inertia locking system” during backslash/door bouncing (if irreversibility option taken):
With the “inertia locking system” being in blocked position and prevents the handle to move in opening position, following requirements need to met in both Y directions:
a)  half-period of 3ms : amplitudes of 150 g and 520 g,
b)  half-period of 5ms : amplitudes of 100g and 350 g,
c)  half-period of 10ms : amplitudes of 80 g and 150 g.
During periods and g stated in a to c, the inertia lock has to remain engaged and prevent any movement of the handle in open position.
II) Real vehicle side impacts pulses validation
As half sinus pulses do not represent quite completely the real vehicle side impact situation, additional requirement is asked with real physical pulses coming from real complete vehicle side impact tests.
The supplier will have to :
- i. Demonstrate a sufficient level of correlation between component simulations and physical tests for previous paragraph half sinus pulses validation (zones A, B, C and their limits)
- ii. Demonstrate with simulation that the inertia locking system blocks and prevents the handle to move in opening position, for 35 pure-Y-acceleration signals available and provided by Stellantis. 31 of these pulses come from physical vehicle tests, and are intended to represent a generic envelope for all real possible behaviours for barrier side impact. 4 of them are combined half-sinus pulses made to highlight negative accelerations dreaded events (counterweight blocking inertia lock, and/or springleaf not catching the inertia lock when it goes back in position)
Physical pulses picture
Half-Sinus combinations
- iii. Demonstrate with simulation that the inertia locking system blocks and prevents the handle to move in opening position, for xx (number to be confirmed, corresponding to side impact loadcases which are taken into account) acceleration signals provided by Stellantis. These additional pulses will come from complete vehicle side impact simulation (at a sufficient mature step of development), and will be provided for each new vehicle project equipped with the handle.
For ii. and iii. demonstrations, the inertia lock has to remain engaged at the end of the pulse application in case of irreversibility function taken.
III) Vibration of spring leaf / irreversible device behavior during crash (if irreversibility function taken with a spring leaf component)
To prevent the dreaded event of spring leaf not being able to catch the inertia lock when the latter comes back in position, the spring leaf shall have a limited vibration behavior during crash.
For that:
a. Spring leaf natural frequency in Hz shall be given for each bracket design (numerical simulation and/or physical test)
b. Inertia lock design shall be oriented to limit the vibration phenomenon and amplitude.
c. For each calculated signal in paragraph I) and II) spring leaf deflection curve shall be indicated, with a deflection < xxxx mm, with the demonstration that this limit is consistent with no risk of inertia lock being unable to catch it.
IV) Mechanical resistance during acceleration tests
For all accelerations validations mentioned in 5.2.4.6.3, no mechanical failure shall occur. (No breakage of counterweight, lever, inertia lock, …)

sections.title: Front and rear impact tests
sections.content:
For the following sinusoidal acceleration applied to the both X directions:
a)  amplitude 40g and 70g continuous (> 80ms): validation by calculation and physical test,
b)  amplitude 50g, 100g and 130g and half-period of 3ms: validation by calculation and physical test,
c)  amplitude 50g and 100g and half-period of 5ms: validation by calculation and physical test,
The handle must not cause cable pull > 4,5mm.
After these tests the handle must be able fully functional and allow door opening.

sections.title: Bezel Lock Cylinder and key cylinder installation
sections.content:
The bracket has a provision to host the lock cylinder for driver door.
There is 1 kind of bezel to cover the lock cylinder:
Without a hole/closed bezel for the key blade
Installation of the key cylinder into the handle shall not be so tight as to require the use of a tool.  The maximum force for insertion of the key cylinder in handle at ambient, shall not exceed 25 N. The key cylinder interface to the handle or bracket assembly shall not rattle or squeak under all test conditions.
All parts are assembled at the STELLANTIS General Assembly Plant

sections.title: Assembly/disassembly requirements of the bezel
sections.content:
The assembly force (at new and at 23°C +/- 3°C) of the cap on the handle, in accordance with -F5, must be less than 40 N per clip per clipping action. (V = 50 mm / min).
Test procedure:
The unclipping force (at new and at 23°C +/- 3°C) of the cap on the handle, in accordance with F5, must be bigger than 20 N and lower than 120 N (V = 50 mm / min) after increased humidity
For unclipping tests, the conditions of increased humidity will be as follows: 10 days at 40˚C and 95% relative humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (in accordance with procedure D471309).
Traction machine equipped with 1 crock, traction successively applied on one side of cap

sections.title: Door cylinder and socket screw/clamp torque test
sections.content:
First point:
The clamp will keep the key cylinder and socket in position after tightening with 4 Nm.
With the COE mounted on a vehicle configuration framework, screws are tightened to 4 Nm +/-15 % (speed: - TBC).
After being put through extreme temperatures of (-40°C and +85°C), i.e. 4 AF cycles in accordance with D47 1309, there can be no loss of coupling greater than 20%
Verification with following procedure (has to be done also post durability test (see chapter 6.6.1.2):
a. Before start of the durability tests, tightening of the screws at the nominal value of 4Nm with a calibrated screw driver
b. Mark (with a line) the angular position of the screw and clamp
c. Mark (with a line) the position of the clamp in the bracket
d. Post-test, measure torque (with same screwdriver) and communicate as well any displacement (difference from b/c) to STELLANTIS engineering. Torque to be in specification (4 Nm +/- 15%).
Key cylinder and or socket to have tight fit.
Second point:
After a minimum of 5 assemblies and 5 disassembly's, we should be able to carry out tightening of screws to a coupling of 4 Nm +/- 15 % (no deterioration of the front and back tappings).
The tests must be done simultaneously on the COE driver side (with lock cylinder housing) and on the COE passenger side (with socket).

sections.title: Key Cylinder Retention
sections.content: When the Key Cylinder is mounted in the handle it must not separate from the handle under following test condition:

sections.title: Key Cylinder Twist-Out Test
sections.content:
A.  Assemble the key cylinder to the handle and the handle to the panel or fixture using the released fasteners. When screws are used, torque them to the minimum specified in the Installation Graphics.
B.  At 23°C, fixture the handle in a vise so that the back of the Key Cylinder can be accessed. Slowly apply the torque to the Key Cylinder via a torque wrench and adaptor that fits over the back of the key cylinder. Apply the torque until failure occurs. Both clockwise and counter clockwise directions must be evaluated
C.  Repeat Steps A & B except with handle and key cylinder assemblies soaked at - 40°C for a minimum of 4 Hours. Either in the chamber, or within 2 minutes of removing the fixture from the chamber repeat step B now at -40°C and record the failure load.
D.  Repeat steps A & B except with handle and key cylinder assemblies soaked at 80°C & 95% Relative Humidity for a minimum of 72 hours. Either in the chamber, or within 2 minutes of removing the fixture from the chamber repeat steps B now at 85°C and record the failure load
The key cylinder retainer must provide enough retention to prevent the key cylinder from twisting out when a minimum torque of 22.6 N-m is applied.

sections.title: Key Cylinder Push Out Test
sections.content:
A.  Assemble the key cylinder to the handle using specified fasteners and torques as from Assembly Graphics.
B.  At 23°C, fixture the handle assembly in the tensile test machine so that the face of the key cylinder is perpendicular to the direction of the applied load.
C.  Apply load with a flat punch with a minimum 12.7 mm diameter centered on the face of the Key Cylinder
D.  Apply load at the rate of 25.4 mm per minute until failure occurs.
E.  Repeat Steps A - D, except with handle and key cylinder assemblies soaked at - 40°C for a minimum of 4 Hours. Either in the chamber, or within 2 minutes of removing the fixture from the chamber repeat steps B - D now at -40°C and record the failure load.
F.  Repeat steps A - D, except with handle and key cylinder assemblies soaked at 80°C & 95% Relative Humidity for a minimum of 72 Hours. Either in chamber, or within 2 minutes of removing the fixture from the chamber repeat steps B - D now at 85°C and record the failure load
The key cylinder retainer must provide enough retention to prevent the key cylinder from being pushed out, when a load of 1112 N is applied.

sections.title: External interfaces requirements
sections.content: See Interface drawing-xxx.pdf (TBD) for all information regarding matching parts and interfaces.

sections.title: Mission profile
sections.content:
Mission profiles for calculations of reliabilities and dependability requirements are stated in chapter 6.4.4.3.1.
Customer specific data are used to define the endurance tests in chapter 6.6.

sections.title: Lifetime
sections.content: No specific requirements

sections.title: Ergonomics and human factors
sections.content:
Surfaces in the visible and perceptible areas of the handle shall be smooth and without streaks, sink marks, discoloration or material traces. Perceptible and visible tool separation burrs and mold mismatches are not permissible. If design surface parting lines are unavoidable, they shall be identified (preferably on verification models). The maximum height of a splitline is 0.1 mm (this can be modified/limited by the appropriate STELLANTIS engineering team, design and quality representatives. In addition the part must be free from traces of mould removal agents which could harm the appearance of the paint.
After the humidity test for 10 days at 40°C and 95% humidity and 2 hours at ambient temperature (following procedure D471309) no deviation/degradation a. to d. accepted.
All hidden components exposed when the handle is actuated shall be black (or alternate color as approved by STELLANTIS).
All visible parts need to meet the graining requirements, outlined in the RFQ Package. Grain application in the tool can be chemical or with electrode, method to be agreed with STELLANTIS engineering and design quality department. Paint specification see HANDLE A9: P MXP_ECH08_1340.
For the handle version with ADML and/or NFC, a non-conductive primer has to be used.

sections.title: Appearance, finish and perceptual quality
sections.content:
All handles, regardless of application, must meet the requirements.
General STELLANTIS requirements concerning burs, injection threshold, clearance, chamfers, coupling radius and stripping:
a.  in norm STELLANTIS A32 4140 “non-specific tolerances - plastic parts “,
b.  in norm STELLANTIS A32 4250 “non-specific tolerances - aluminum alloy parts”.

sections.title: Gap between handle cover and bezel
sections.content:
The gap between the short cover handle and the bezel should be linear with handle in rest position and in open position (gap 0,5 mm +/- 0,2mm). Contact of both parts are not permitted because of risk of scratches and noise development.
This requirement is valid for the following overall temperature/humidity range:
a) new parts
b) after 24h @ -40°C
c) after 24h @ 85°C
d) after 24h @ 40°C / 95 % r.h.
Supplier to provide dimensional stack up in order to proof capability.

sections.title: Operating force in F1
sections.content:
A physical test during the RFQ and/or development, and when the maturity of the part is sufficient, will be done by Stellantis with the Supplier support with a mock up in a vehicle in order to define the definitive effort range/curves (min- max- nominal value) for rocking/paddle handle solution.
The mock-up will be presented to a group of different people (man, women, ect..) representing the final customer.
The vehicle will represent different configuration in order to consider, among others, different ground height and door joint reaction.
The operating forces will also consider the crash constraints.
For handle pull force directions to apply, F1 direction (see graph 6.0)
For the tests, apply a constant load of 35N applied on the cable following F10 to simulate (see picture 5.6)
the side door latch.  In these conditions, the draw force of the handle until to the end position must meet the force displacement curves of the graph below:
Graph 6.0
Interpretation of the force displacement curve :
The green is the target value to meet, development target for bracket/handle system. Update of the values after PV testing and agreement with STELLANTIS engineering. The blue- and orange Curves correspond to the Tolerance Range of the Operating Force, it includes all tolerances (dimension/spring torque on the single components). As well, manufacturing/assembly variations. This Tolerance Range should be used for capability determination of the Operating Force.
The deviation of the measure forces must not be higher than 5N and a vertical variation (between a maximum and a minimum or a minimum and maximum in succession) of 2N maximum can be tolerated.
Graph 6.1
After passing through a climate chamber at 110°C for ½ an hour and being returned to ambient temperature, the force curve must remain in conformity with the starting requirements.
The moving grip part of the handle must firmly return to its rest position under all circumstances, regardless of temperature and humidity impacts.

sections.title: RAMS requirements
sections.content:
The Feared events are classified according to their severity grade: The severity Grade represents the effect of the Feared event in terms of injury or inconvenience for the user.
Grade
Definition
Comments
G1
Dissatisfaction or deterioration of a functionality of the vehicle.
General performances of the vehicle preserved. The user can continue to use his vehicle. There is no necessity of a repair.
G2
Loss of a functionality of the vehicle.
Appearance of embarrassing symptoms. The user can continue to use his vehicle, but a repair is essential to make quickly.
G3
The vehicle is unavailable for the user.
Impossibility of beginning a distance or
Impossibility of finishing a distance due to loss of a main function or due to non observance of the regulation (risk of infringement).
Impossibility of parking the vehicle in its current state (risk of inviolability).
G4
Risk of physical injuries for human.
Can potentially lead to traffic accident or physical injuries.

sections.title: Protection against hostility
sections.content:
The handle system incl. the key cylinder has to meet the Thatcham requirement.
In case of a non passed Thatcham Attack test, Supplier is requested to work closly with STELLANTIS Engineering and the Lock cylinder Supplier in order to meet the requirements.
All attack test to be performed at Room Temperature and test set up to be reviewed before testing with STELLANTIS engineering. Handle/bracket assembly with key cylinder mounted on a door sking or sheet metal piece.
a.  All parts tested for strength will be tested to failure at the specified temperature.
b.  Handle loads at failure must meet or exceed specified strength test load.
c.  Mode of failure and test load at failure must be documented.

sections.title: Axial Loading
sections.content:
Drive a screw into the cylinder and apply the load to the screw as explained below in Picture 6.2:
The cylinder retention strength when tested with an impact load to simulate Thatcham slide hammer testing shall be a minimum of 1.5kg dropped repetitively from a height of 0.5m. 10 Cycles to perform, as well test to failure. Cylinder to be retained in bracket.
Picture 6.2

sections.title: Push in Test
sections.content: The cylinder when mounted in a production door environment at STELLANTIS shall withstand two minutes of brutal force when hammered with a 12mm diameter rod or screwdriver. Criteria:  Cylinder shall remain in the bracket.

sections.title: Tong Load
sections.content: Attack with a tong the housing of the key cylinder (bezel removed) and apply a torque of 60Nm clockwise and counter clockwise 10 times. The cylinder remains into the bracket.

sections.title: Bending Test
sections.content:
Use a hammer to pound a screwdriver into the cylinder.
1.  Apply a 300 N downward load.
2.  Apply a 300 N upward load.
3.  Repeat steps 1-2 for a total of 20 cycles.
Cylinder has to remain in the bracket.

The HANDLE must correspond to the requirements formulated in the technical specs ST Materials PMXP.
The marking of the parts must be compliant with the norms CS-11000
Each part must be marked to allow to identify the material for the recycling (if case the dimension of the part allow it).
The Material base must be compliant to the norms STELLANTIS B20 1315, B20 1415, B62 0020.
Because of different definitions of handles and brackets, the marking of the STELLANTIS references is not required in the tool.
Therefore it is asked that the marking of the references is done during the assembly sequence (micro-percussion, ink jet, laser) on the bracket and on the handle (finished good: with/without thief shield, with/without guidance cone, with/without anti-G system,…).

sections.title: Before start of testing:
sections.content:
Measure following performance criterias :
a.  Pull force until to full open position
b.  Free play in +/-X, +/- Y and +/-Z direction
c.  Cable release stroke
d.  Proof Sensor function (if ADML Version)
e.  Proof NFC function (If ADML/NFC content)

sections.title: During testing:
sections.content:
Measure all criterias in chapter 6.6.1.3.1
a.  At the end of the first half of the total number of cycles a deviation of 10% of the force in the original release stroke/force diagram is permitted in accordance with F1. No functioning noise/malfunction is permitted.  No malfunction is permitted.
b.  At the end of the test, a deviation of 20% of the force in the original release stroke/force graph is permitted in accordance with F1.
c.  By the end of the endurance test, the handle has to go from fully draw position to its rest position by the mechanic of the bracket in stand alone part condition.
d.  Free play in all directions can increase by 5% (compare with measured values before test)
e.  The inertia lock function shall not have any mal function.
For each of the Check performance criteria actions (listed in the previous table) a report has to be provided for all tests.
At the end of testing, 100% of the system should remain operational.

sections.title: After regular cycle testing:
sections.content:
After finishing the complete cycle test listed in chapter 6.6.1.2 and in order to allow an evaluation of the performance of the controls by a statistical method, the handle has to be tested to failure. Conditions at 23°C and ambient humidity.
Beyond the number of cycles listed in table 6.3, checks to be performed every 10,000 cycles.
End of Life test to stop if test shows one of the following failure criteria:
a)  The cable release stroke is out of specification (see chapter 6.2.4.2).
b)  A reduction of activation force in the original release stroke/force graph of 30% or an increase of this force of 75% is considered a failure.
c)  the number of cycles reaches 1.5 times the total number of cycles selected in chapter 6.6.1.1.
At the end of the test, a complete overview of test reports (focused on failures) as well as their potential consequences is carried out and submitted to STELLANTIS engineering.

The angular deviations of the lock cylinder position on the bracket are less than 1° in relation to the values at new conditions. These values are inspected every 5,000 cycles.

Set the measuring instrument according to this starting "door lock" position
Perform the lock-unlock cycles
Misure the angular deviation of the "door lock" (max 1° along the key axis) respect to the starting position
If a batch of 3 parts passes the total number of cycles as described above, the test is considered as being satisfactory.

sections.title: Cycle testing with parts out of mass production
sections.content: Test definition same as in 6.2.4.1 but number of samples can be extended up to 20 depend on assembly line design (EOL test, manual assembly i.e.). Number of parts to be tested to agree with STELLANTIS engineering.

sections.title: Corrosion testing
sections.content:
The handle has to fulfill all requirements in the accelerated cyclic corrosion test, in the vehicle position, in accordance with the std. CS.00251 (120 cycles according to std. SAE-J-2334)
Measure following performance criteria before start of test:
a.  Pull force until to full open position.
b.  Free play in +/-X, +/- Y and +/-Z direction
c.  Cable release stroke
d.  Proof Sensor function (if ADML Version)
e.  Proof NFC function (If ADML + NFC content)
f.  Function inertia blocker
Test procedure
Per Lab Procedure the components are assembled to a vehicle or e-coated and painted sheet metal pocket fixture that represents door sheet metal. The door handle assembly shall be placed in car position in the chamber. If the inertial lock rotates during a 1.52 m/s door slam, or during each handle actuation, then it can be rotated this same angular rotation during each corrosion cycle. Handle shall be cycled once per day during testing to simulate usage and avoid seizing that would not represent normal usage.
Acceptance Criteria
Door Handle Assembly must meet the requirements of CS.00251, with regards to function and appearance. Handles must not lose function during or after the test. The Inertia Lock must return to its normal position within 1 second when held in car position and only under the influence of its return spring, after it’s actuated to its minimum and full engagement positions. The handle and all of its movable components shall return to their normal positions within 1 second when held in car position only under the influence of its return spring when actuated to 50% and 100% of full travel.

sections.title: Electronic/mechanical Testing
sections.content: Electromechanical testing: see B21 7120 and B21 7130

sections.title: Appendix A - Assembly of the handle at STELLANTIS
sections.content:
POSITIONING INTERNAL HANDLE BRACKET INTO THE DOOR :
The handle bracket must have a robust pre-holding system for handle assembly
The clipping force must not exceed 4 daN with a one hand
The handle bracket and handle assembly must be fitted without an alignment jig
The clips must allow the bracket pre-assembly as per procedure requested/shared with Stellantins assembly team. The shape of the locking clip must not cause a hard point (hall effect) for clipping (the clip must slide over the metal sheet during the rotation)
CABLE LATCH CLIPPING ON DOOR HANDLE BRACKET
The overlenght of the cable latch must be long enough for connection to the handle bracket (lever and fixed interface, otherwise accessibility for clipping must be guaranteed. The interface must be as close as possible to the door opening.
The cable latch must have its clips in the Y direction on the handle bracket
The cable latch must have only one possible position on the handle bracket

sections.title: Appendix B - Detailed Protocol of Noise testing (example)
Documents/ information necessary
sections.content:
- The type of assembly and minimum fastening torque of the part on the car.
- An Excel file for each acoustic signal is required (duration of a signal: Ca. 100s)
- An Excel file for the ageing signal (about a total of 117 hours)
- An acceptance graph.
- The supplier's inspection report of the units and sub-assemblies tested (with the reference(s) of the parts used)

sections.title: Methods necessary
sections.content:
a)  An anechoic or semi-anechoic room
b)  A three-axle vibrator which can be insulated so that noise pollution does not occur
c)  One single HANDLE (prototype stage) or a door equipped with window-opener, window, slide and window sealing strip (VRS stage)
d)  A bench on which the HANDLE can be supported in car-representative configuration and which is adapted for vibratory tests (first mode higher than 100Hz) (prototype stage)
e)  A bench on which the door + HANDLE assembly can be supported in car-representative configuration and which is adapted to vibratory tests (first mode higher than 100Hz) (prototype stage) (VRS stage)
f)  Two micros (without wind-breaker ball) with a range of function corresponding to the required frequency band and which are calibrated (e.g. : G.R.A.S type 40AER - ½'' free field, pre-polarized, of a sensibility of 50 mV/Pa, class I).
The first micro is placed as close as possible to the part (this is not necessarily a criteria of acceptance, but allows for better communication on the type of noise recorded between the different parties)
g)  The second micro is placed at a representative distance from distance LV / ears of the passenger who is closest in the car configuration (here ca. 70 cm). This micro is the one applicable for all sound level measurements.

sections.title: First time, acoustic characteristics of test conditions
sections.content:
The objective is to play interfering noise acoustical signals under the required test conditions (vibrato is working + HANDLE support HANDLE in the “measuring” room), but without the HANDLE.
Each acoustical signal is played one by one (the number of signals may vary from 5 to 7, and they have a duration of ca. 100s).
The 3 axes can be played simultaneously or separately. In the case that the signals are played direction by direction, this will increase the number of interceptions.
100s x 5 track x 3 directions = 1500s
The acoustic signals are submitted in a separate document in the form of an Excel file and can be improved in the course of the project (supplier has to ask for this before running any tests)
At the same time, recording is started for each of the micros (Acquisition of acoustic pressure by one-third octave via the micro(s) within the frequency range of [20 HZ ; 20 000 HZ] then compared to the acceptance graph.    The test technician is present to do a subjective hearing test and give his opinion if he should detect a noise coming from the installation required for testing.

sections.title: Second time, acoustic characteristics of the HANDLE body when new
sections.content:
(to be carried out when the first test is validated)
Install the new HANDLE in the previous assembly.
Play each of the acoustic signals one after the other as done in the first time' test.
At the same time, recording is started for each of the micros (Acquisition of acoustic pressure by third octave filters via the micro(s) within the frequency range of [20 HZ; 20 000 HZ] then compared to the acceptance graph.    The test technician is present to do a subjective hearing test and give his opinion if he should detect a noise coming from the installation required for testing.

sections.title: Third time; ageing of the HANDLE due to operation.
sections.content:
Subject the HANDLE to a climatic handling endurance test of 3 years.
The part undergoes the same ageing as the one subjected to the classic handling endurance test, but endurance will be stopped on this part for an ageing period equivalent to 3 years.

sections.title: Fourth time; HANDLE acoustic charact. after endurance operation of 3 years
sections.content:
Play each of the acoustic signals on this HANDLE aged by handling one after the other as done in the second time' test.
At the same time, recording is started for each of the micros (Acquisition of acoustic pressure by one-third octave via the micro(s) within the frequency range of [20 HZ; 20 000 HZ] then compared to the acceptance graph.    The test technician is present to do a subjective hearing test and give his opinion if he should detect a noise coming from the installation required for testing.

sections.title: Fifth time; ageing of the HANDLE during driving (at ambient temperature)
sections.content:
Apply a vibratory ageing sequence on this HANDLE which is already aged from handling.
The temporary signals of ageing of a duration of 117 hours will be sent to you separately from this document as an Excel file and can be improved/amended in the course of the project.
The 3 axes must be played simultaneously; this profile equalling a car driving period of 3 years.
During this ageing process, the part is positioned in the same way as on vehicle.

sections.title: Sixth time; acoustic characteristics of the aged HANDLE
sections.content:
Play each of the acoustic signals with the opener having been subjected to handling endurance and vibratory testing as run with times 2 and 4.
At the same time, recording is started for each of the micros (Acquisition of acoustic pressure by one-third octave via the micro(s) within the frequency range of [20 HZ; 20 000 HZ] then compared to the acceptance graph.    The test technician is present to do a subjective hearing test and give his opinion if he should detect a noise coming from the installation required for testing.

sections.title: Principe of test
sections.content:
Before engagement of the freezing test, the request is to prepare the tested parts by engagement of 4 AF cycles (to prepare the parts to receive the water ingress).
Once this preliminary test has been carried out, the tests consist of undergoing a sequence of tests in 3 stages with at the end of each of them a status on the functionality of the ODH.
The supplier need to make pictures on critical zone to clearly identify the potential area how the ice could be damaged the part ( functionality of it) pictures done before test and just after extraction of the climatic chamber . And supplier need to check and measure the part as soon as the  part is extracted to the climatic chamber.

sections.title: Test mean
sections.content:
It is constituted:
a water supply
a partial mean of testing including the definition of the door sheet metal in the area around the ODH
a spacious climatic chamber to accommodate the part on partial mean and allowing the temperature T = - 40 °C to be reached

sections.title: Mode operational
sections.content:
The ODH must undergo each of the following 4 cold tests in order and on the same part (see § Dry part, § After icing, § Runoff and § Karcher washing). At the end of each of them, the characteristics specified in the reference document are verified. (list of functionality and specific measurements checked to be propose and validated with STLA team)
Phase 1: DRY PART
The ODH fixed on its partial mean in car position is placed in a climatic chamber leveled off beforehand with - 40 °C and must remain 2 hours there
Phase 2: AFTER ICING
The ODH having undergone the test in § Dry room, is exposed to ambient temperature (see: 23°C ± 5°C) until droplets form.
Under these conditions, it is subject to the following constraints:
• The “ODH + his support” assembly is introduced into a climatic chamber which has previously been stabilized at -20°C for 1 hour.
• Immediately, the refrigeration system and ventilation are stopped.
• At -5°C, the temperature is stabilized and the “ODH on its support” assembly remains in the enclosure for 3 hours
Phase 3: WATER INGRESS, RUN-OFF
At the conclusion of the tests of the § After icing, to leave the unit the climatic test rig and to let the whole return to the room temperature of workshop (see: 23 °C ± 5 °C) then to carry out the following tests:
• The ODH is exposed during 5 Hours under a jet of water having the following characteristics:
• flow: 10 l/hour without any pressure
• watering system reproducing rain positioned vertically on the ODH (ODH in vehicle position) and watering covering 100% of the surface at the ODH; watering drain positioned 300 mm from the ODH (vertical to it).
• To carry out the tests of the § After freezing test
